name: FKT Gnarly
classification_notes: The name and style suggest a display-leaning grotesque sans; the weight range from Light to Black with italics is consistent with a contemporary grotesque family.
summary: A ten-style grotesque sans from Formerly Known, spanning Light to Black with matching italics throughout.
description: FKT Gnarly is a grotesque sans-serif offered in five weights from Light to Black, each paired with an italic, giving ten styles in total. The family is sold under Formerly Known's Inclusive License, which covers print and digital use under a single EULA priced by company size. The naming suggests a typeface with some personality beyond the purely neutral grotesque.
foundry_blurb: Formerly Known offer FKT Gnarly as a ten-style family with a 50% discount on the full family purchase. It is licensed under their Inclusive License, a single all-encompassing EULA designed to cover print, digital and everything in between, with pricing tiers based on company size rather than use case restrictions.

name: FKT Rhosyn
classification_notes: The name and foundry context suggest a serif design; without additional specimen detail, a transitional serif classification is the most defensible based on typical offerings from this foundry.
summary: A single-weight serif from Formerly Known, sold under the foundry's all-inclusive licensing model.
description: FKT Rhosyn is currently available in a Regular weight, offering a straightforward entry point for text and editorial applications. Formerly Known's Inclusive License covers print, digital and everything between, with pricing scaled to company size rather than usage type. Limited specimen information is available on the purchase page.
foundry_blurb: Formerly Known sell FKT Rhosyn under their Inclusive License, a single EULA covering print, digital and all uses in between, priced by company size with no unnecessary restrictions or usage limits.

name: FKT Rag
classification_notes: The name and weight range suggest a contemporary grotesque or humanist sans-serif; without further specimen detail the broader sans-serif grotesque category is most appropriate.
summary: A seven-weight sans-serif from Formerly Known, spanning Light to XBold under a single all-inclusive licence structure.
description: FKT Rag is offered in seven weights from Light through to XBold, with no italics or condensed variants listed. Formerly Known distribute it under their Inclusive License, a single EULA covering print, digital and everything between, priced by company size rather than use case. The family purchase offers a 50% discount on the sum of individual styles.
foundry_blurb: Formerly Known offer FKT Rag as a seven-style family, available individually or at a 50% discount as a complete family purchase. It is sold under their Inclusive License, a simplified all-encompassing EULA with no unnecessary restrictions, priced in tiers based on the end user's company size.

name: FKT Echo
classification_notes: The family spans four width variants from standard to extra condensed, with four weights each, suggesting a utilitarian grotesque designed for flexible typographic use across varied measures.
summary: A four-weight grotesque available across four width variants, from standard to extra condensed, covering a wide range of typographic applications.
description: Sixteen styles span four widths from standard to Extra Condensed, each available in Regular, Medium, Bold and XBold. The family is built for flexibility, allowing the same typographic voice to carry across headlines, tight columns and display settings. Licensing is handled through Formerly Known's single inclusive EULA, tiered by company size.
foundry_blurb: FKT Echo is available as a full 16-style family at 50% off the sum of individual styles, or as individual fonts across four width variants and four weights. Formerly Known offer it under their Inclusive License, a single EULA covering print, digital and everything in between, with pricing based on the end user's company size.

name: FKT Sinvoll
classification_notes: The family offers both sans and serif monospaced cuts, each with a stencil variant, placing it squarely in the mono category with display characteristics introduced by the stencil styles.
summary: A four-style monospaced family from Formerly Known offering both sans and serif cuts, each available in a stencil variant.
description: FKT Sinvoll pairs sans and serif monospaced designs under one collection, with stencil editions of each adding a harder, more graphic edge. All four styles share Regular weight only, keeping the family tight and intentional rather than sprawling. The stencil cuts give it range across technical documents and poster work.
foundry_blurb: FKT Sinvoll is available as a collection of four styles at a 50% discount over individual purchases. The family comprises FKT Sinvoll Sans Mono Regular, FKT Sinvoll Sans Mono Stencil Regular, FKT Sinvoll Serif Mono Regular and FKT Sinvoll Serif Mono Stencil Regular, licensed under Formerly Known's Inclusive License, which covers print, digital and everything in between with pricing based on company size.

name: FKT Ashmore
classification_notes: The naming conventions and weight range suggest a contemporary grotesque or neo-grotesque sans-serif, consistent with Formerly Known's general output, though the page provides no explicit classification.
summary: A nine-weight sans-serif family from Formerly Known, spanning Thin to Black with an inclusive, size-based licensing model.
description: The family runs nine weights from Thin through to Black, with intermediate cuts including Book and XBold for granular typographic control. Formerly Known license it under a single all-encompassing EULA covering print, digital and beyond, with pricing scaled by company size rather than use case. The page provides limited detail on stylistic intent or script coverage beyond the weight range itself.
foundry_blurb: Formerly Known offer FKT Ashmore as a nine-style family purchasable individually or at a 50% discount as a complete family. It is licensed under their Inclusive License, a single EULA designed to cover all common use cases without unnecessary restrictions, priced by company size.
